title: The Money Conversation I Haven't Had with My Parents
subtitle: A journal prompt set for naming what needs to be said about money before it becomes an emergency
excerpt: A journal set for the money conversations that sit in the background of family relationships, unsaid. Five prompts moving from what is currently happening to what conversation would clear it.
sourceNotes: Original journal prompt set written for homemade.education.

practice.whenToUse: Use when the financial dimension of the parent relationship feels unspoken, tangled, or building toward a point where clarity will be needed.

content.text: A journal set for the money conversations that haven't happened yet but probably need to. The prompts move from what is currently unsaid, to the fear underneath the silence, to what a clear conversation might look like.
How does money currently sit between you and your parents?
Write for three minutes without editing. Guilt, obligation, gratitude, resentment, fear — all of it is useful data.
Name it as plainly as you can. The sentence that would be too much to say at Christmas dinner. Get it onto the page.
What are you afraid would happen if the conversation happened?
Name the specific fear: that they would feel let down, that they would be angry, that you would have to say no, that you would discover something you don't want to know.
What is the cost of not having it?
What is running in the background while the conversation doesn't happen? Write what it costs in stress, in money, in the relationship.
What is the first sentence of the conversation you'd need to have?
Just the first sentence. Specific, direct, kind. Write three versions and choose the one that says what needs to be said.
